diagrams.title: CFL Pumping Lemma String Decomposition
diagrams.description: Visualizes the five-part decomposition of a string in a context-free language. Shows how any sufficiently long string s (where |s| ≥ p) can be split into u, v, w, x, y with specific constraints: |vwx| ≤ p and |vx| ≥ 1. This is the foundation for understanding how pumping works in CFLs.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the structural decomposition requirement of the CFL Pumping Lemma theorem

diagrams.title: Pumping Operation Visualization
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the pumping process showing how strings u(v^i)w(x^i)y are generated for different values of i (i=0,1,2...). Shows that all pumped strings must remain in the language L. This visualizes the core property that v and y can be repeated (or removed) while maintaining language membership.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the iterative pumping property that defines context-free languages

diagrams.title: Proof by Contradiction: Non-CFL Example
diagrams.description: Shows a typical proof structure using pumping lemma to prove a language is not context-free. Uses the classic example L = {a^n b^n c^n | n ≥ 1} or L = {ww | w ∈ Σ*}. Demonstrates how pumping leads to a contradiction when the pumped string violates the language's structure.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how the pumping lemma is applied to prove languages are not context-free
